ogTitle: Lilyvale vs Limeburners Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lilyvale and Limeburners Creek.
heading: Lilyvale vs Limeburners Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lilyvale, New South Wales and Limeburners Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lilyvale has a lower overall climate risk score (45/100) compared to Limeburners Creek (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lilyvale or Limeburners Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lilyvale has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Limeburners Creek scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lilyvale scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Limeburners Creek scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lilyvale or Limeburners Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lilyvale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
